title: UniSuper to snub Firmus float over lack of detail
full_text:
UniSuper, the country's fourth-largest pension fund, has cast doubt on the highly anticipated initial public offering of Firmus Technologies and claims valuing it at $15.5 billion, warning it would not be investing in the data centre developer as too little was known about its underlying business.
John Pearce, the chief investment officer of UniSuper, which manages $150 billion in retirement savings, said he also did not expect a public float of Firmus in the near term, marking a rare public expression of scepticism about the company's plans.
Firmus, which has won backing from global chipmaker Nvidia, and fund managers such as Regal Funds Management, is mooted to list in the last quarter of this year. "Do I see an IPO anytime soon? I doubt it," said Pearce. "I don't think that their non-deal roadshow was well received in Australia."
Nonetheless, on Wednesday, The Australian Financial Review's Street Talk column reported that Nvidia had emerged as the biggest investor in Firmus Technologies' latest $US2 billion ($2.9 billion) equity raising, which is set to almost double the group's value to $15.5 billion before its ASX listing.
Firmus, which develops and maintains data centres specialising in providing artificial intelligence services to clients such as Facebook-owner Meta Platforms, conducted a roadshow this year with investors.
It said it planned to have $30 billion of committed annual revenue when it goes back to investors in late September with its IPO plans.
However, a number of investors have privately criticised a lack of clarity in Firmus' presentation on how it would reach its ambitious $30 billion revenue target, and wanted additional information before taking a view on its IPO.
Companies p15
Fairfax Media Management Pty Limited
author: Lucas Baird, Jenny Wiggins and Alex
heading: Superannuation and Investments
